title: Neogaerrhinum kelloggii
intro: Neogaerrhinum kelloggii, also known as Kellogg's gilia, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Neogaerrhinum kelloggii include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Neogaerrhinum kelloggii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infused in teas and tinctures, and is sometimes used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
